items.english: I’m curious about that balloon. What does it mean?
items.korean: 저 풍선이 궁금해. 무슨 뜻이니?

items.english: It means you must not enter.
items.korean: 들어오면 안 된다는 뜻이야.

sections.section_name: Reading: A Picture Letter from a Mother
sections.description: The story of a drawing letter sent by an illiterate woman to her husband

items.english: This letter was written in 1973 by a woman whose husband was far away.
items.korean: 이 편지는 남편이 멀리 떨어져 있던 한 여인에 의해 1973년에 쓰였다.

items.english: If I got a letter from you, I would be very happy.
items.korean: 만약 당신에게서 편지를 받는다면 난 정말 행복할 거예요.

items.english: I had two workers prepare our field and plant seeds.
items.korean: 인부 두 명을 시켜 우리 밭을 준비하고 씨를 심게 했어요.

items.english: I would be so happy if you were with me.
items.korean: 네가 나와 함께 있다면 정말 행복할 텐데.
items.grammar_point: Subjunctive Past (If + S + Past Verb, S + would + V)

items.english: It was written in 1973 by a woman whose husband was far away.
items.korean: 그것은 남편이 멀리 떨어져 있는 한 여인에 의해 1973년에 쓰였다.

items.english: I’m curious about sign language. What does it mean?
items.korean: 나는 수화에 대해 궁금해. 그것은 무엇을 의미하니?
items.structure: I'm curious about / What does it mean?

grammar_properties.target_pattern: I'm curious about / What does it mean?

items.english: It means “Hello”.
items.korean: 그것은 “안녕”을 의미해.

items.english: What does this sign mean? It means you must not ride a bike here.
items.korean: 이 표지판은 무엇을 의미하니? 여기서 자전거를 타면 안 된다는 뜻이야.
items.structure: What does ... mean? / you must not ...

syntax_chunks.eng: What does this sign mean? It means
you must not ride a bike here.

grammar_properties.target_pattern: What does ... mean? / you must not ...

items.english: This house is famous for having thousands of secrets in it.
items.korean: 이 집은 수천 개의 비밀을 간직하고 있는 것으로 유명하다.

syntax_chunks.eng: having thousands of secrets in it.

items.english: Speaking to family in a foreign country is rather easy and simple today.
items.korean: 오늘날 외국에 있는 가족에게 말하는 것은 꽤 쉽고 간단하다.

syntax_chunks.eng: Speaking to family in a foreign
country is rather easy and simple today.

items.english: At the time, more than 5% of the people in Italy could not read or write.
items.korean: 당시에 이탈리아 인구의 5% 이상이 읽거나 쓸 줄 몰랐다.

syntax_chunks.eng: At the time, more than 5% of the
people in Italy could not read or write.

items.english: My dear love
items.korean: I miss you so much, and I reach my arms out toward you.,사랑하는 당신, 당신이 정말 보고 싶어서 당신을 향해 팔을 뻗어 봅니다.,G0507

items.english: If I got a letter from you
items.korean: I would be very happy.,당신에게서 편지를 받는다면 정말 행복할 거예요.,G0508,가정법 과거

syntax_chunks.kor: 편지를 받는다면 정말 행복할 거예요.,G0508,가정법 과거

items.english: Your mother fell ill and I’m going to visit her in the hospital.
items.korean: 당신의 어머니께서 병환이 나셔서 제가 병원에 면회를 가려고 합니다.
items.structure: fall ill / visit in the hospital

syntax_chunks.eng: Your mother fell ill and I’m
going to visit her in the hospital.

grammar_properties.target_pattern: fall ill / visit in the hospital

items.english: I’ll go there with our middle son while the oldest looks after the youngest.
items.korean: 첫째가 막내를 돌보는 동안 나는 둘째 아들과 그곳에 가겠습니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: I’ll go there with our middle son
while the oldest looks after the youngest.

items.english: I hired a man whose sons are good workers.
items.korean: 나는 아들들이 일을 잘하는 한 남자를 고용했습니다.

items.english: I got enough oil to fill a large pot and a small one.
items.korean: 나는 큰 단지와 작은 단지를 채울 만큼 충분한 기름을 얻었습니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: I got enough oil to fill
a large pot and a small one.

items.english: But whether one or the other wins
items.korean: it’s the same.,그러나 이쪽이 이기든 저쪽이 이기든 그것은 마찬가지입니다.,G0513

items.english: I would be so happy if you were with me.
items.korean: 당신이 나와 함께 있다면 정말 행복할 거예요.,G0508,가정법 과거

items.english: It was written in 1973 by a woman whose husband was far away.
items.korean: 그것은 남편이 멀리 떨어져 있던 한 여인에 의해 1973년에 쓰였다.

syntax_chunks.eng: a woman whose husband was far away.

items.english: Pictures speak a thousand words
items.korean: you know.,알다시피 그림은 천 마디 말보다 더 많은 것을 전달하죠.,G0514

syntax_chunks.kor: 마디 말보다 더 많은 것을 전달하죠.,G0514

items.english: Lala is the girl whose dog wants a hot dog.
items.korean: 라라는 개가 핫도그를 원하는 소녀이다.

items.english: If I had wings
items.korean: I would fly in the sky.,나에게 날개가 있다면 하늘을 날 수 있을 텐데.,G0508,가정법 과거

items.english: If I were a bird

items.english: The animal whose neck is long is a giraffe.
items.korean: 목이 긴 동물은 기린이다.

items.english: Speaking to family members or friends in a foreign country is rather easy and simple today.
items.korean: 오늘날 타국에 있는 가족이나 친구에게 말을 거는 것은 꽤 쉽고 간단합니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: Speaking to family members or friends in a
foreign country is rather easy and simple today.
syntax_chunks.kor: 말을 거는 것은 꽤 쉽고 간단합니다.

items.english: But before the days of phones and the Internet, it was not that easy.
items.korean: 하지만 전화와 인터넷이 없던 시절에는 그리 쉬운 일이 아니었습니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: But before the days of phones and
the Internet, it was not that easy.

items.english: People just sent a letter and waited for a reply for weeks.
items.korean: 사람들은 단지 편지를 보내고 수주 동안 답장을 기다렸습니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: People just sent a letter and
waited for a reply for weeks.

items.english: And it was a lot harder if they couldn’t read or write.
items.korean: 그리고 그들이 읽거나 쓸 수 없다면 그것은 훨씬 더 힘든 일이었습니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: And it was a lot harder
if they couldn’t read or write.
syntax_chunks.kor: 없다면 그것은 훨씬 더 힘든 일이었습니다.

items.english: This letter shows how people got over these difficulties.
items.korean: 이 편지는 사람들이 이러한 어려움을 어떻게 극복했는지 보여줍니다.

items.english: She lived in Sicily, an Italian island, while her husband worked in Germany.
items.korean: 그녀는 이탈리아의 한 섬인 시칠리아에 살았고, 그녀의 남편은 독일에서 일했습니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: She lived in Sicily, an Italian
island, while her husband worked in Germany.

items.english: This letter was discovered by Sicilian writer Gesualdo Bufalino.
items.korean: 이 편지는 시칠리아 작가 제수알도 부팔리노에 의해 발견되었습니다.

items.english: We are all in good health except for the little one.
items.korean: 우리 모두는 막내를 빼고는 건강합니다.
items.structure: If I got ... I would / look after

syntax_chunks.eng: health except for the little one.

grammar_properties.target_pattern: If I got ... I would / look after

items.english: He’s a little sick, but not seriously.
items.korean: 그는 약간 아프지만 심각하지는 않습니다.
items.structure: If I got ... I would / look after

grammar_properties.target_pattern: If I got ... I would / look after

items.english: I already sent you a letter, but there was no reply, so I am sad about it.
items.korean: 내가 이미 당신에게 편지를 보냈지만 답장이 없어서 슬퍼요.
items.structure: If I got ... I would / look after

items.english: We picked lots of olives from our olive trees this year.
items.korean: 올해 우리는 올리브 나무에서 많은 올리브를 땄습니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: from our olive trees this year.

items.english: He knocked the olives down, and his two sons helped him, picking them up from the ground.
items.korean: 그가 올리브를 털었고, 그의 두 아들이 바닥에서 그것들을 줍는 것을 도왔습니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: He knocked the olives down, and his two
sons helped him, picking them up from the ground.
syntax_chunks.kor: 아들이 바닥에서 그것들을 줍는 것을 도왔습니다.

items.english: I paid him 27,000 lire for the work.
items.korean: 나는 그 일의 보수로 그에게 2만 7천 리레를 주었습니다.

items.english: I spent 12,000 more for the olive press.
items.korean: 올리브 압착기를 위해 1만 2천 리레를 더 썼습니다.

items.english: I can sell it at a price of 1,300 lire a liter.
items.korean: 나는 그것을 리터당 1,300리레의 가격으로 팔 수 있습니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: I can sell it at a

items.structure: so many seats that / whether A or B

items.korean: 이탈리아 공산당은 의석을 너무 많이 잃어서 거의 사멸한 것 같습니다.
items.structure: so many seats that / whether A or B

items.english: Nothing changes for us poor people.
items.korean: 우리 가난한 사람들을 위해 바뀌는 것은 아무것도 없습니다.
items.structure: so many seats that / whether A or B

grammar_properties.target_pattern: so many seats that / whether A or B

items.english: We worked yesterday, and we will work again tomorrow.
items.korean: 우리는 어제 일했고 내일 또 일할 것입니다.
items.structure: so many seats that / whether A or B

grammar_properties.target_pattern: so many seats that / whether A or B

items.english: My love, my heart thinks of you as Christmas is coming.
items.korean: 내 사랑, 크리스마스가 다가오니 내 마음은 당신 생각뿐입니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: of you as Christmas is coming.

items.english: We all miss you so much.
items.korean: 우리 모두 당신을 너무 그리워합니다.

items.english: I’m sending you a big hug from me and our three little kids.
items.korean: 나와 세 명의 어린 아이들의 큰 포옹을 당신에게 보냅니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: I’m sending you a big hug
from me and our three little kids.

items.english: Goodbye, dear love.
items.korean: 안녕, 사랑하는 당신.

items.english: My heart is yours, joined to you as our two rings are.
items.korean: 내 마음은 우리의 두 반지처럼 당신과 하나인 당신의 것입니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: My heart is yours, joined to
you as our two rings are.

items.english: Witch 1:  Frog Prince, you say?
items.korean: 마녀 1: 개구리 왕자라고요?

items.english: You don’t look like a frog.
items.korean: 당신은 개구리처럼 보이지 않습니다.

items.english: Well, it doesn’t matter.
items.korean: 글쎄, 그것은 중요하지 않습니다.

items.english: If you’re a prince, you’re a prince.
items.korean: 당신이 왕자라면 당신은 왕자입니다.

items.english: And I won’t let you save Snow White.
items.korean: 그리고 백설공주를 구하게 놔두지 않을 거예요.

items.english: Here, eat the rest of this apple.
items.korean: 자, 이 사과의 남은 부분을 먹어보세요.

items.english: Prince:  No, thank you.
items.korean: 프린스: 아니요, 고마워요.

items.english: That’s not what I want!
items.korean: 그건 내가 원하는 게 아니야!
items.structure: that + S + V / N + that / Wh-word

grammar_properties.target_pattern: that + S + V / N + that / Wh-word

items.english: into    run away Witch 1:  (Coming out of the house) Hi, Prince.
items.korean: 도망가다 마녀 1: (집에서 나오며) 안녕 왕자님.

syntax_chunks.eng: (Coming out of the house) Hi, Prince.

items.english: How are you feeling?
items.korean: 기분이 어때요?

items.english: Prince:   I’m glad to meet you.
items.korean: 프린스: 만나서 반가워요.

items.english: I’m the Frog Prince.
items.korean: 나는 개구리 왕자다.

items.english: I hope you can turn me back into a frog so I can live happily ever after.
items.korean: 저를 다시 개구리로 바꿔서 오래오래 행복하게 살 수 있기를 바랍니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: I hope you can turn me back into
a frog so I can live happily ever after.

items.english: 82 Special Lesson ₖ [Page 82] (Prince and Witch 2 come in.) Prince:  Ms.
items.korean: 82 특별과 ₖ [82 페이지] (왕자와 마녀 2가 들어옵니다.) Prince: Ms.

syntax_chunks.eng: and Witch 2 come in.) Prince:  Ms.

items.english: Witch 2:  If you’re a frog, I’m the King of France.
items.korean: 마녀 2: 당신이 개구리라면 나는 프랑스의 왕이다.

syntax_chunks.eng: frog, I’m the King of France.

items.english: Prince:  No, I’m not a frog.
items.korean: 왕자: 아니요, 저는 개구리가 아닙니다.

items.english: But I need a witch to turn me back into a frog so I can live happily ever after.
items.korean: 하지만 내가 영원히 행복하게 살 수 있도록 나를 개구리로 되돌려줄 마녀가 필요해요.

syntax_chunks.eng: But I need a witch to turn me back
syntax_chunks.kor: 하지만 내가 영원히 행복하게 살 수

syntax_chunks.eng: into a frog so I can live happily ever after.
syntax_chunks.kor: 있도록 나를 개구리로 되돌려줄 마녀가 필요해요.

items.english: Witch 2:  Let’s talk about it inside.
items.korean: 마녀 2: 내부에서 얘기해보자.

items.english: I will serve you a delicious lunch.
items.korean: 맛있는 점심을 대접하겠습니다.

items.english: Prince:  Thank you for inviti ng me.
items.korean: 프린스: 초대해 주셔서 감사합니다.

items.english: Oh, this house is made of cookies and candies.
items.korean: 아, 이 집은 쿠키와 사탕으로 이루어져 있어요.

items.english: Do you know any children by the name of Hansel and Gretel?
items.korean: 헨젤과 그레텔이라는 이름의 아이들을 아시나요?

syntax_chunks.eng: Do you know any children by
the name of Hansel and Gretel?

items.english: Witch 2:  Yes, Prince, I do.
items.korean: 마녀 2: 네, 왕자님, 그렇죠.

items.english: (With her mouth watering) They are not fat enough yet, but you are ....
items.korean: (군침을 흘리며) 아직 충분히 뚱뚱하지는 않은데 당신은....

syntax_chunks.eng: (With her mouth watering) They are not
fat enough yet, but you are ....

items.english: I am the Frog Prince.
items.korean: 나는 개구리 왕자다.

items.english: Could you turn me back into a frog so I can live happily ever after?
items.korean: 나를 다시 개구리로 바꿔서 영원히 행복하게 살 수 있게 해주시겠어요?

syntax_chunks.eng: Could you turn me back into a
frog so I can live happily ever after?

items.english: Fairy:  Well, I’m on my way to see Cinderella, but I’ll give it a try.
items.korean: 요정: 음, 신데렐라를 보러 가는 길이지만 한 번 가볼게요.

syntax_chunks.eng: Fairy:  Well, I’m on my way to
see Cinderella, but I’ll give it a try.

items.english: It’s my fi  rst time, you know.
items.korean: 나 이번이 처음이야, 알잖아.

items.english: ( Fairy turns Prince into a wheel.) Oops!
items.korean: (요정은 왕자를 바퀴로 변신시킵니다.) 이런!

items.english: Sorry, but don’t worry.
items.korean: 미안하지만 걱정하지 마세요.

items.english: Everything will be okay.
items.korean: 모든 것이 괜찮을 것입니다.

items.english: Prince:  (To himself) Oh, what a fool I’ve been!
items.korean: 프린스: (혼자서) 아, 정말 바보같았어!

items.english: I want to be sitting at home with the Princess, living happily ever after.
items.korean: 나는 공주님과 함께 집에 앉아 행복하게 살고 싶습니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: I want to be sitting at home
with the Princess, living happily ever after.

items.english: But instead, I’m stuck here under this cart and I’ll live unhappily ever after.
items.korean: 하지만 나는 여기 이 수레 밑에 갇혀서 영원히 불행하게 살게 될 것입니다.

syntax_chunks.eng: But instead, I’m stuck here under this
syntax_chunks.kor: 하지만 나는 여기 이 수레 밑에

syntax_chunks.kor: 갇혀서 영원히 불행하게 살게 될 것입니다.

items.english: (The clock hits twelve, and the wheel turns into Prince.) stuck    unhappily Prince:  I can’t believe this.
items.korean: (시계가 12시를 가리키고 바퀴가 프린스로 변합니다.) 불행하게도 갇혔습니다. 프린스: 믿을 수가 없어요.

stuck    unhappily Prince:  I can’t believe this.
